001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
024
025import java.lang.annotation.*;
026import java.lang.reflect.*;
027import java.util.*;
028import java.util.function.*;
029
030import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.marshall.bson.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.marshall.cbor.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.marshall.csv.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.marshall.hjson.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.marshall.hocon.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.marshall.html.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.marshall.ini.*;
042import org.apache.juneau.marshall.jcs.*;
043import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
044import org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonl.*;
045import org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonschema.*;
046import org.apache.juneau.marshall.markdown.*;
047import org.apache.juneau.marshall.msgpack.*;
048import org.apache.juneau.marshall.oapi.*;
049import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parquet.*;
050import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
051import org.apache.juneau.marshall.plaintext.*;
052import org.apache.juneau.marshall.prototext.*;
053import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
054import org.apache.juneau.marshall.soap.*;
055import org.apache.juneau.marshall.toml.*;
056import org.apache.juneau.marshall.uon.*;
057import org.apache.juneau.marshall.urlencoding.*;
058import org.apache.juneau.marshall.xml.*;
059import org.apache.juneau.marshall.yaml.*;
060
061/**
062 * Base class for all Context beans.
063 *
064 * <p>
065 * Context beans follow the convention of havinTg the following parts:
066 * <ul>
067 *    <li>A {@link Builder} class for configuring the context bean.
068 *    <ul>
069 *       <li>This bean is non-thread-safe and meant for one-time use.
070 *    </ul>
071 *    <li>A {@link Context#Context(Builder)} constructor that takes in a builder object.
072 *    <ul>
073 *       <li>This bean is thread-safe and cacheable/reusable.
074 *    </ul>
075 *    <li>A {@link ContextSession} class for doing work.
076 *    <ul>
077 *       <li>This bean is non-thread-safe and meant for one-time use.
078 *    </ul>
079 *
080 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
081 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
082 * </ul>
083 *
084 */
085@SuppressWarnings({
086   "unchecked",
087   "rawtypes",
088   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
089   "java:S3740"  // Raw Builder types in reflection-based context creation; parameterization is not possible at call sites
090})
091public abstract class Context {
092
093   // Property name constants
094   private static final String PROP_annotations = "annotations";
095   private static final String PROP_debug = "debug";
096
097   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
098   private static final String ARG_type = "type";
099   private static final String ARG_builder = "builder";
100   private static final String ARG_copyFrom = "copyFrom";
101   private static final String ARG_work = "work";
102   private static final String ARG_subtype = "subtype";
103   private static final String ARG_c = "c";
104   private static final String ARG_values = "values";
105   private static final String ARG_from = "from";
106
107   /**
108    * Builder class.
109    */
110   @SuppressWarnings({
111      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
112   })
113   public abstract static class Builder<SELF extends Builder<SELF>> {
114
115      /**
116       * Returns this object cast to the self (CRTP) type.
117       *
118       * @return This object.
119       */
120      protected final SELF self() {
121         return (SELF)this;
122      }
123
124
125      private boolean debug;
126      private final AnnotationWorkList applied = AnnotationWorkList.create();
127      private Cache<HashKey,? extends Context> cache;
128      private Class<? extends Context> type;
129      private Context impl;
130      private List<Annotation> annotations;
131      private final List<Object> builders = list();
132
133      /**
134       * Constructor.
135       * Default settings.
136       */
137      protected Builder() {
138         debug = env("Context.debug", false);
139         annotations = list();
140         registerBuilders(this);
141
142         // By default, the type being created should be the class declaring the builder.
143         var dc = getClass().getDeclaringClass();
144         if (Context.class.isAssignableFrom(dc))
145            type((Class<? extends Context>)dc);
146      }
147
148      /**
149       * Copy constructor.
150       *
151       * @param copyFrom The builder to copy from.
152       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
153       */
154      protected Builder(Builder<?> copyFrom) {
155         assertArgNotNull(ARG_copyFrom, copyFrom);
156         annotations = copyOf(copyFrom.annotations);
157         debug = copyFrom.debug;
158         type = copyFrom.type;
159         registerBuilders(this);
160      }
161
162      /**
163       * Copy constructor.
164       *
165       * @param copyFrom The bean to copy from.
166       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
167       */
168      protected Builder(Context copyFrom) {
169         assertArgNotNull(ARG_copyFrom, copyFrom);
170         annotations = copyOf(copyFrom.annotations);
171         debug = copyFrom.debug;
172         type = copyFrom.getClass();
173         registerBuilders(this);
174      }
175
176      /**
177       * Defines annotations to apply to specific classes and methods.
178       *
179       * <p>
180       * Allows you to dynamically apply Juneau annotations typically applied directly to classes and methods.
181       * Useful in cases where you want to use the functionality of the annotation on beans and bean properties but
182       * do not have access to the code to do so.
183       *
184       * <p>
185       * As a rule, any Juneau annotation with an <l>on()</l> method can be used with this setting.
186       *
187       * <p>
188       * The following example shows the equivalent methods for applying the {@link Marshalled @Marshalled} annotation:
189       * <p class='bjava'>
190       *    <jc>// Class with explicit annotation.</jc>
191       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(properties=<js>"street,city,state"</js>)
192       *    <jk>public class</jk> A {...}
193       *
194       *    <jc>// Class with annotation applied via @MarshalledConfig</jc>
195       *    <jk>public class</jk> B {...}
196       *
197       *    <jc>// Java REST method with @MarshalledConfig annotation.</jc>
198       *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>(...)
199       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(on=<js>"B"</js>, properties=<js>"street,city,state"</js>)
200       *    <jk>public void</jk> doFoo() {...}
201       * </p>
202       *
203       * <p>
204       * In general, the underlying framework uses this method when it finds dynamically applied annotations on
205       * config annotations.  However, concrete implementations of annotations are also provided that can be passed
206       * directly into builder classes like so:
207       * <p class='bjava'>
208       *    <jc>// Create a concrete @Marshalled annotation.</jc>
209       *    <ja>Marshalled</ja> <jv>annotation</jv> = MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(B.<jk>class</jk>).properties(<js>"street,city,state"</js>).build();
210       *
211       *    <jc>// Apply it to a serializer.</jc>
212       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer.<jsm>create</jsm>().annotations(<jv>annotation</jv>).build();
213       *
214       *    <jc>// Serialize a bean with the dynamically applied annotation.</jc>
215       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> B());
216       * </p>
217       *
218       * <p>
219       * The following is the list of annotations builders provided that can be constructed
220       * and passed into the builder class:
221       * <ul class='javatreec'>
222       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledAnnotation}
223       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanCtorAnnotation}
224       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanIgnoreAnnotation}
225       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledIgnoreAnnotation}
226       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanPropAnnotation}
227       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledPropAnnotation}
228       *    <li class='ja'>{@link ExampleAnnotation}
229       *    <li class='ja'>{@link NamePropertyAnnotation}
230       *    <li class='ja'>{@link ParentPropertyAnnotation}
231       *    <li class='ja'>{@link SwapAnnotation}
232       *    <li class='ja'>{@link UriAnnotation}
233       *    <li class='ja'>{@link CsvAnnotation}
234       *    <li class='ja'>{@link HtmlAnnotation}
235       *    <li class='ja'>{@link JsonAnnotation}
236       *    <li class='ja'>{@link SchemaAnnotation}
237       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MsgPackAnnotation}
238       *    <li class='ja'>{@link OpenApiAnnotation}
239       *    <li class='ja'>{@link PlainTextAnnotation}
240       *    <li class='ja'>{@link SoapXmlAnnotation}
241       *    <li class='ja'>{@link UonAnnotation}
242       *    <li class='ja'>{@link UrlEncodingAnnotation}
243       *    <li class='ja'>{@link XmlAnnotation}
244       * </ul>
245       *
246       * <p>
247       * The syntax for the <l>on()</l> pattern match parameter depends on whether it applies to a class, method, field, or constructor.
248       * The valid pattern matches are:
249       * <ul class='spaced-list'>
250       *  <li>Classes:
251       *       <ul>
252       *          <li>Fully qualified:
253       *             <ul>
254       *                <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass"</js>
255       *             </ul>
256       *          <li>Fully qualified inner class:
257       *             <ul>
258       *                <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass$Inner1$Inner2"</js>
259       *             </ul>
260       *          <li>Simple:
261       *             <ul>
262       *                <li><js>"MyClass"</js>
263       *             </ul>
264       *          <li>Simple inner:
265       *             <ul>
266       *                <li><js>"MyClass$Inner1$Inner2"</js>
267       *                <li><js>"Inner1$Inner2"</js>
268       *                <li><js>"Inner2"</js>
269       *             </ul>
270       *       </ul>
271       *    <li>Methods:
272       *       <ul>
273       *          <li>Fully qualified with args:
274       *             <ul>
275       *                <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass.myMethod(String,int)"</js>
276       *                <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass.myMethod(java.lang.String,int)"</js>
277       *                <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass.myMethod()"</js>
278       *             </ul>
279       *          <li>Fully qualified:
280       *             <ul>
281       *                <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass.myMethod"</js>
282       *             </ul>
283       *          <li>Simple with args:
284       *             <ul>
285       *                <li><js>"MyClass.myMethod(String,int)"</js>
286       *                <li><js>"MyClass.myMethod(java.lang.String,int)"</js>
287       *                <li><js>"MyClass.myMethod()"</js>
288       *             </ul>
289       *          <li>Simple:
290       *             <ul>
291       *                <li><js>"MyClass.myMethod"</js>
292       *             </ul>
293       *          <li>Simple inner class:
294       *             <ul>
295       *                <li><js>"MyClass$Inner1$Inner2.myMethod"</js>
296       *                <li><js>"Inner1$Inner2.myMethod"</js>
297       *                <li><js>"Inner2.myMethod"</js>
298       *             </ul>
299       *       </ul>
300       *    <li>Fields:
301       *       <ul>
302       *          <li>Fully qualified:
303       *             <ul>
304       *                <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass.myField"</js>
305       *             </ul>
306       *          <li>Simple:
307       *             <ul>
308       *                <li><js>"MyClass.myField"</js>
309       *             </ul>
310       *          <li>Simple inner class:
311       *             <ul>
312       *                <li><js>"MyClass$Inner1$Inner2.myField"</js>
313       *                <li><js>"Inner1$Inner2.myField"</js>
314       *                <li><js>"Inner2.myField"</js>
315       *             </ul>
316       *       </ul>
317       *    <li>Constructors:
318       *       <ul>
319       *          <li>Fully qualified with args:
320       *             <ul>
321       *                <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass(String,int)"</js>
322       *                <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass(java.lang.String,int)"</js>
323       *                <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass()"</js>
324       *             </ul>
325       *          <li>Simple with args:
326       *             <ul>
327       *                <li><js>"MyClass(String,int)"</js>
328       *                <li><js>"MyClass(java.lang.String,int)"</js>
329       *                <li><js>"MyClass()"</js>
330       *             </ul>
331       *          <li>Simple inner class:
332       *             <ul>
333       *                <li><js>"MyClass$Inner1$Inner2()"</js>
334       *                <li><js>"Inner1$Inner2()"</js>
335       *                <li><js>"Inner2()"</js>
336       *             </ul>
337       *       </ul>
338       *    <li>A comma-delimited list of anything on this list.
339       * </ul>
340       *
341       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
342       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig}
343       * </ul>
344       *
345       * @param values
346       *    The annotations to register with the context.
347       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
348       * @return This object.
349       */
350      public SELF annotations(Annotation...values) {
351         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
352         annotations(l(values));
353         return self();
354      }
355
356      /**
357       * Same as {@link #annotations(Annotation...)} but uses a list as input.
358       *
359       * @param values
360       *    The annotations to register with the context.
361       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or contain <jk>null</jk> values.
362       * @return This object.
363       */
364      public SELF annotations(List<Annotation> values) {
365         annotations.addAll(assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values));
366         return self();
367      }
368
369      /**
370       * Applies a set of applied to this builder.
371       *
372       * <p>
373       * An {@link AnnotationWork} consists of a single pair of {@link AnnotationInfo} that represents an annotation instance,
374       * and {@link AnnotationApplier} which represents the code used to apply the values in that annotation to a specific builder.
375       *
376       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
377       * <p class='bjava'>
378       *    <jc>// A class annotated with a config annotation.</jc>
379       *    <ja>@MarshalledConfig</ja>(unsortedProperties=<js>"$S{unsortedProperties,false}"</js>)
380       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyClass {...}
381       *
382       *    <jc>// Find all annotations that themselves are annotated with @ContextPropertiesApply.</jc>
383       *    Stream&lt;AnnotationInfo&lt;?&gt;&gt; <jv>annotations</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>).getAnnotations().stream().filter(<jsf>CONTEXT_APPLY_FILTER</jsf>);
384       *    VarResolverSession <jv>vrs</jv> = VarResolver.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.createSession();
385       *    AnnotationWorkList <jv>work</jv> = AnnotationWorkList.of(<jv>vrs</jv>, <jv>annotations</jv>);
386       *
387       *    <jc>// Apply any settings found on the annotations.</jc>
388       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
389       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
390       *       .apply(<jv>work</jv>)
391       *       .build();
392       * </p>
393       *
394       * @param work The list of annotations and appliers to apply to this builder.
395       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
396       * @return This object.
397       */
398      public SELF apply(AnnotationWorkList work) {
399         assertArgNotNull(ARG_work, work);
400         applied.addAll(work);
401         work.forEach(x -> builders.forEach(x::apply));
402         return self();
403      }
404
405      /**
406       * Same as {@link #applyAnnotations(Object...)} but explicitly specifies a class varargs to avoid compilation warnings.
407       *
408       * @param from The classes or methods on which the annotations are defined.
409       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
410       * @return This object.
411       */
412      public SELF applyAnnotations(Class<?>...from) {
413         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_from, from);
414         return applyAnnotations((Object[])from);
415      }
416
417      /**
418       * Applies any of the various <ja>@XConfig</ja> annotations on the specified classes or methods to this context.
419       *
420       * <p>
421       * Any annotations found that themselves are annotated with {@link ContextApply} will be resolved and
422       * applied as properties to this builder.  These annotations include:
423       * <ul class='javatreec'>
424       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig}
425       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BsonConfig}
426       *    <li class='ja'>{@link CborConfig}
427       *    <li class='ja'>{@link CsvConfig}
428       *    <li class='ja'>{@link HtmlConfig}
429       *    <li class='ja'>{@link HtmlDocConfig}
430       *    <li class='ja'>{@link HjsonConfig}
431       *    <li class='ja'>{@link HoconConfig}
432       *    <li class='ja'>{@link IniConfig}
433       *    <li class='ja'>{@link JsonConfig}
434       *    <li class='ja'>{@link JsonlConfig}
435       *    <li class='ja'>{@link JsonSchemaConfig}
436       *    <li class='ja'>{@link JcsConfig}
437       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarkdownConfig}
438       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MsgPackConfig}
439       *    <li class='ja'>{@link OpenApiConfig}
440       *    <li class='ja'>{@link ParserConfig}
441       *    <li class='ja'>{@link ParquetConfig}
442       *    <li class='ja'>{@link PrototextConfig}
443       *    <li class='ja'>{@link PlainTextConfig}
444       *    <li class='ja'>{@link SerializerConfig}
445       *    <li class='ja'>{@link SoapXmlConfig}
446       *    <li class='ja'>{@link TomlConfig}
447       *    <li class='ja'>{@link UonConfig}
448       *    <li class='ja'>{@link UrlEncodingConfig}
449       *    <li class='ja'>{@link XmlConfig}
450       *    <li class='ja'>{@link YamlConfig}
451       *    <li class='ja'><c>RdfConfig</c>
452       * </ul>
453       *
454       * <p>
455       * Annotations on classes are appended in the following order:
456       * <ol>
457       *    <li>On the package of this class.
458       *    <li>On interfaces ordered parent-to-child.
459       *    <li>On parent classes ordered parent-to-child.
460       *    <li>On this class.
461       * </ol>
462       *
463       * <p>
464       * Annotations on methods are appended in the following order:
465       * <ol>
466       *    <li>On the package of the method class.
467       *    <li>On interfaces ordered parent-to-child.
468       *    <li>On parent classes ordered parent-to-child.
469       *    <li>On the method class.
470       *    <li>On this method and matching methods ordered parent-to-child.
471       * </ol>
472       *
473       * <p>
474       * The default var resolver {@link VarResolver#DEFAULT} is used to resolve any variables in annotation field values.
475       *
476       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
477       * <p class='bjava'>
478       *    <jc>// A class annotated with a config annotation.</jc>
479       *    <ja>@MarshalledConfig</ja>(unsortedProperties=<js>"$S{unsortedProperties,false}"</js>)
480       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyClass {...}
481       *
482       *    <jc>// Apply any settings found on the annotations.</jc>
483       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
484       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
485       *       .applyAnnotations(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>)
486       *       .build();
487       *
488       *    <jc>// A method annotated with a config annotation.</jc>
489       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyClass {
490       *       <ja>@MarshalledConfig</ja>(unsortedProperties=<js>"$S{unsortedProperties,false}"</js>)
491       *       <jk>public void</jk> myMethod() {...}
492       *    }
493       *
494       *    <jc>// Apply any settings found on the annotations.</jc>
495       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
496       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
497       *       .applyAnnotations(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getMethod(<js>"myMethod"</js>))
498       *       .build();
499       * </p>
500       *
501       * @param from The classes or methods on which the annotations are defined.
502       *    Can be any of the following types:
503       *    <ul>
504       *       <li>{@link Class}
505       *       <li>{@link ClassInfo}
506       *       <li>{@link Method}
507       *       <li>{@link MethodInfo}
508       *    <li>A collection/stream/array of anything on this list.
509       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
510       * @return This object.
511       */
512      public SELF applyAnnotations(Object...from) {
513         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_from, from);
514         var work = AnnotationWorkList.create();
515         Arrays.stream(from).forEach(x -> traverse(work, x));
516         return apply(work);
517      }
518
519      /**
520       * Traverses the specified object and adds applicable annotations to the work list.
521       *
522       * @param work The annotation work list to add to.
523       * @param x The object to traverse (Class, ClassInfo, Method, or MethodInfo).
524       * @return The work list.
525       */
526      private static AnnotationWorkList traverse(AnnotationWorkList work, Object x) {
527         var ap = AP;
528         CollectionUtils.traverse(x, y -> {
529            if (x instanceof Class<?> x2)
530               work.add(rstream(ap.find(info(x2))).filter(CONTEXT_APPLY_FILTER));
531            else if (x instanceof ClassInfo x2)
532               work.add(rstream(ap.find(x2)).filter(CONTEXT_APPLY_FILTER));
533            else if (x instanceof Method x2)
534               work.add(rstream(ap.find(info(x2))).filter(CONTEXT_APPLY_FILTER));
535            else if (x instanceof MethodInfo x2)
536               work.add(rstream(ap.find(x2)).filter(CONTEXT_APPLY_FILTER));
537            else
538               illegalArg("Invalid type passed to applyAnnotations:  {0}", cn(x));
539         });
540         return work;
541      }
542
543      /**
544       * Returns this builder cast to the specified subtype if it is an instance of that type.
545       *
546       * <p>
547       * This is a type-safe way to check if this builder is an instance of a specific builder subtype
548       * and cast it accordingly. Returns an empty {@link Optional} if this builder is not an instance
549       * of the specified subtype.
550       *
551       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
552       * <p class='bjava'>
553       *    Builder <jv>b</jv> = JsonSerializer.<jsm>create</jsm>();
554       *    Optional&lt;JsonSerializer.Builder<?>&gt; <jv>jsonBuilder</jv> = <jv>b</jv>.asSubtype(JsonSerializer.Builder.<jk>class</jk>);
555       *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>jsonBuilder</jv>.isPresent()) {
556       *       <jc>// Use JsonSerializer.Builder<?>-specific methods</jc>
557       *       <jv>jsonBuilder</jv>.get().pretty();
558       *    }
559       * </p>
560       *
561       * @param <T> The builder subtype.
562       * @param subtype The builder subtype class to cast to.
563       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
564       * @return An {@link Optional} containing this builder cast to the subtype, or empty if not an instance.
565       */
566      public <T extends Builder<?>> Optional<T> asSubtype(Class<T> subtype) {
567         return opt(assertArgNotNull(ARG_subtype, subtype).isInstance(this) ? subtype.cast(this) : null);
568      }
569
570      /**
571       * Build the object.
572       *
573       * @return The built object.
574       */
575      public Context build() {
576         return innerBuild();
577      }
578
579      /**
580       * Convenience method for calling {@link #build()} while avoiding a cast.
581       *
582       * @param <T> The type to cast the built object to.
583       * @param c The type to cast the built object to.
584       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
585       * @return The built context bean.
586       */
587      public final <T extends Context> T build(Class<T> c) {
588         if (type == null || ! assertArgNotNull(ARG_c, c).isAssignableFrom(type))
589            type = c;
590         return (T)innerBuild();
591      }
592
593      /**
594       * Specifies a cache to use for hashkey-based caching.
595       *
596       * <p>
597       * When a cache is specified, contexts with the same hash key will be reused from the cache
598       * instead of creating new instances.  This improves performance when building multiple contexts
599       * with identical configurations.
600       *
601       * <p>
602       * If <jk>null</jk> is specified, caching is disabled and each call to {@link #build()} will
603       * create a new context instance.
604       *
605       * @param value The cache.
606       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (disables caching, each build creates a new instance).
607       * @return This object.
608       */
609      public SELF cache(Cache<HashKey,? extends Context> value) {
610         cache = value;
611         return self();
612      }
613
614      /**
615       * Returns <jk>true</jk> if any of the annotations/appliers can be applied to this builder.
616       *
617       * @param work The work to check.
618       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
619       * @return <jk>true</jk> if any of the annotations/appliers can be applied to this builder.
620       */
621      public boolean canApply(AnnotationWorkList work) {
622         return assertArgNotNull(ARG_work, work).stream().anyMatch(x -> builders.stream().anyMatch(x::canApply));
623      }
624
625      /**
626       * Copy creator.
627       *
628       * @return A new mutable copy of this builder.
629       */
630      public abstract SELF copy();
631
632      /**
633       * <i><l>Context</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Debug mode.
634       *
635       * <p>
636       * Enables the following additional information during serialization:
637       * <ul class='spaced-list'>
638       *    <li>
639       *       When bean getters throws exceptions, the exception includes the object stack information
640       *       in order to determine how that method was invoked.
641       *    <li>
642       *       Enables {@link MarshallingTraverseContext.Builder#detectRecursions()}.
643       * </ul>
644       *
645       * <p>
646       * Enables the following additional information during parsing:
647       * <ul class='spaced-list'>
648       *    <li>
649       *       When bean setters throws exceptions, the exception includes the object stack information
650       *       in order to determine how that method was invoked.
651       * </ul>
652       *
653       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
654       * <p class='bjava'>
655       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with debug enabled.</jc>
656       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
657       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
658       *       .debug()
659       *       .build();
660       *
661       *    <jc>// Create a POJO model with a recursive loop.</jc>
662       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
663       *       <jk>public</jk> Object <jf>f</jf>;
664       *    }
665       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MyBean();
666       *    <jv>bean</jv>.<jf>f</jf> = <jv>bean</jv>;
667       *
668       *    <jc>// Throws a SerializeException and not a StackOverflowError</jc>
669       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jv>bean</jv>);
670       * </p>
671       *
672       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
673       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#debug()}
674       *    <li class='jm'>{@link ContextSession.Builder#debug(Boolean)}
675       * </ul>
676       *
677       * @return This object.
678       */
679      public SELF debug() {
680         return debug(true);
681      }
682
683      /**
684       * Same as {@link #debug()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
685       *
686       * @param value The value for this setting.
687       * @return This object.
688       */
689      public SELF debug(boolean value) {
690         debug = value;
691         return self();
692      }
693
694      /**
695       * Returns all the annotations that have been applied to this builder.
696       *
697       * @return All the annotations that have been applied to this builder.
698       */
699      public AnnotationWorkList getApplied() { return applied; }
700
701      /**
702       * Returns the context class that this builder should create.
703       *
704       * @return The context class if it was specified.
705       */
706      public Optional<Class<?>> getType() { return opt(type); }
707
708      /**
709       * Returns the hashkey of this builder.
710       *
711       * <p>
712       * Used to return previously instantiated context beans that have matching hashkeys.
713       * The {@link HashKey} object is suitable for use as a hashmap key of a map of context beans.
714       * A context bean is considered equivalent if the {@link HashKey#equals(Object)} method is the same.
715       *
716       * @return The hashkey of this builder.
717       */
718      public HashKey hashKey() {
719         return HashKey.of(debug, type, annotations);
720      }
721
722      /**
723       * Specifies a pre-instantiated bean for the {@link #build()} method to return.
724       *
725       * <p>
726       * If a non-null value is provided and it's an instance of the context type, {@link #build()} will return
727       * that instance instead of creating a new one.  If <jk>null</jk>, the normal build process continues.
728       *
729       * @param value The value for this setting.
730       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (normal build process will continue).
731       * @return This object.
732       */
733      public SELF impl(Context value) {
734         impl = value;
735         return self();
736      }
737
738      /**
739       * Returns <jk>true</jk> if debug is enabled.
740       *
741       * @return <jk>true</jk> if debug is enabled.
742       */
743      public boolean isDebug() { return debug; }
744
745      /**
746       * Associates a context class with this builder.
747       *
748       * <p>
749       * This is the type of object that this builder creates when the {@link #build()} method is called.
750       *
751       * <p>
752       * By default, it's the outer class of where the builder class is defined.
753       *
754       * <p>
755       * If <jk>null</jk> is set, {@link #build()} will throw an exception.  The default constructor automatically
756       * sets this to the outer class, so <jk>null</jk> should only be set explicitly if you want to override the default.
757       *
758       * @param value The context class that this builder should create.
759       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will cause {@link #build()} to throw an exception).
760       * @return This object.
761       */
762      public SELF type(Class<? extends Context> value) {
763         type = value;
764         return self();
765      }
766
767      /**
768       * Registers the specified secondary builders with this context builder.
769       *
770       * <p>
771       * When {@link #apply(AnnotationWorkList)} is called, it gets called on all registered builders.
772       *
773       * @param values The builders to add to the list of builders.
774       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
775       */
776      protected void registerBuilders(Object...values) {
777         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
778         for (var b : values) {
779            if (b == this)
780               builders.add(b);
781            else if (b instanceof Builder b2)
782               builders.addAll(b2.builders);
783            else
784               builders.add(b);
785         }
786      }
787
788      private ConstructorInfo getContextConstructor() {
789         return CONTEXT_CONSTRUCTORS.get(type, getClass());
790      }
791
792      private Context innerBuild() {
793         if (type == null)
794            throw rex("Type not specified for context builder {0}", cn(getClass()));
795         if (nn(impl) && type.isInstance(impl))
796            return type.cast(impl);
797         if (nn(cache))
798            return cache.get(hashKey(), () -> getContextConstructor().newInstance(this));
799         return getContextConstructor().newInstance(this);
800      }
801   }
802
803   /*
804    * Cache of static <c>create</c> methods that return builder instances for context classes.
805    *
806    * <p>
807    * This cache stores {@link MethodInfo} objects for public static methods named <c>create</c> that return
808    * builder objects. The methods are discovered by:
809    * <ol>
810    *    <li>Finding public constructors that take a single parameter (the builder type)
811    *    <li>Looking for a matching static <c>create</c> method that returns the builder type
812    *    <li>Caching the result for future lookups
813    * </ol>
814    *
815    * <p>
816    * Used by {@link #createBuilder(Class)} to efficiently locate and invoke builder creation methods.
817    *
818    * @see #createBuilder(Class)
819    */
820   private static final Cache<Class<?>,MethodInfo> BUILDER_CREATE_METHODS = Cache.<Class<?>,MethodInfo>create()
821      .supplier(type -> {
822         var c = info(type);
823         // @formatter:off
824         return c.getPublicConstructors().stream()
825            .filter(ci -> ci.hasNumParameters(1) && ! ci.getParameter(0).getParameterType().is(type))
826            .map(ci -> c.getPublicMethod(
827               x -> x.isStatic()
828               && x.isNotDeprecated()
829               && x.hasName("create")
830               && x.hasReturnType(ci.getParameter(0).getParameterType())
831               ).orElse(null))
832            .filter(Objects::nonNull)
833            .findFirst()
834            .orElseThrow(() -> rex("Could not find builder create method on class {0}", cn(type)));
835         // @formatter:on
836      })
837      .build();
838
839
840   /*
841    * Cache of public constructors on context classes that accept builder instances.
842    *
843    * <p>
844    * This cache stores {@link ConstructorInfo} objects for public constructors on context classes that take
845    * a single parameter of the builder type. The constructor is discovered by:
846    * <ol>
847    *    <li>Finding public constructors on the context type that take exactly one parameter
848    *    <li>Matching constructors where the parameter type is a parent of (or equal to) the builder type
849    *    <li>Caching the result for future lookups
850    * </ol>
851    *
852    * <p>
853    * Used by {@link Builder#getContextConstructor()} to efficiently locate and invoke context constructors
854    * when building context instances from builders.
855    *
856    * @see Builder#getContextConstructor()
857    * @see Builder#innerBuild()
858    */
859   private static final Cache2<Class<? extends Context>,Class<? extends Builder>,ConstructorInfo> CONTEXT_CONSTRUCTORS = Cache2.<Class<? extends Context>,Class<? extends Builder>,ConstructorInfo>create()
860      .supplier((cacheType, builderType) -> {
861         var ct = info(cacheType);
862         var bt = info(builderType);
863         return ct
864            .getPublicConstructor(x -> x.hasNumParameters(1) && x.getParameter(0).getParameterType().isAssignableFrom(builderType))
865            .orElseThrow(() -> rex("Public constructor not found: {0}({1})", ct.getName(), bt.getName()));
866      })
867      .build();
868
869   /*
870    * Default annotation provider instance for finding annotations on classes, methods, fields, and constructors.
871    *
872    * <p>
873    * This is a static reference to {@link AnnotationProvider#INSTANCE}, used by the {@link Builder#traverse(AnnotationWorkList, Object)}
874    * method to discover annotations that can be applied to context builders.
875    *
876    * <p>
877    * The annotation provider supports:
878    * <ul>
879    *    <li>Finding annotations on classes, methods, fields, and constructors
880    *    <li>Traversing class hierarchies (parent-to-child or child-to-parent order)
881    *    <li>Supporting runtime annotations (annotations added programmatically)
882    *    <li>Caching results for performance
883    * </ul>
884    *
885    * @see AnnotationProvider
886    * @see Builder#traverse(AnnotationWorkList, Object)
887    */
888   private static final AnnotationProvider AP = AnnotationProvider.INSTANCE;
889
890   /**
891    * Predicate for annotations that themselves are annotated with {@link ContextApply}.
892    *
893    * <p>
894    * Also matches {@link BeanConfig} from {@code juneau-commons}, which intentionally lacks a
895    * {@link ContextApply @ContextApply} meta-annotation because {@code juneau-commons} cannot reference
896    * marshall-layer types. {@link AnnotationWorkList} routes {@link BeanConfig} to the
897    * {@link BeanConfigAnnotation} applier via an explicit special case.
898    */
899   public static final Predicate<AnnotationInfo<?>> CONTEXT_APPLY_FILTER = x -> x.hasAnnotation(ContextApply.class) || x.annotationType() == BeanConfig.class;
900
901   /**
902    * Instantiates a builder of the specified context class.
903    *
904    * <p>
905    * Looks for a public static method called <c>create</c> that returns an object that can be passed into a public
906    * or protected constructor of the class.
907    *
908    * @param type The builder to create.
909    * @return A new builder.
910    */
911   public static Builder createBuilder(Class<? extends Context> type) {
912      assertArgNotNull(ARG_type, type);
913      try {
914         return ((Builder)BUILDER_CREATE_METHODS.get(type).invoke(null)).type(type);
915      } catch (ExecutableException e) {
916         throw toRex(e);
917      }
918   }
919
920
921   private final AnnotationProvider annotationProvider;
922   private final boolean debug;
923   private final List<Annotation> annotations;
924
925   /**
926    * Constructor for this class.
927    *
928    * @param builder The builder for this class.
929    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
930    */
931   protected Context(Builder builder) {
932      assertArgNotNull(ARG_builder, builder);
933      init(builder);
934      annotations = copyOf(builder.annotations);
935      annotationProvider = AnnotationProvider.create().addRuntimeAnnotations(annotations).build();
936      debug = builder.debug;
937   }
938
939   /**
940    * Copy constructor.
941    *
942    * @param copyFrom The context to copy from.
943    */
944   protected Context(Context copyFrom) {
945      annotationProvider = copyFrom.annotationProvider;
946      annotations = copyOf(copyFrom.annotations);
947      debug = copyFrom.debug;
948   }
949
950   /**
951    * Creates a builder from this context object.
952    *
953    * <p>
954    * Builders are used to define new contexts (e.g. serializers, parsers) based on existing configurations.
955    *
956    * @return A new Builder object.
957    */
958   public Builder copy() {
959      throw unsupportedOp();
960   }
961
962   /**
963    * Create a session builder based on the properties defined on this context.
964    *
965    * <p>
966    * Use this method for creating sessions where you want to override basic settings.
967    * Otherwise, use {@link #getSession()} directly.
968    *
969    * @return A new session builder.
970    */
971   public ContextSession.Builder createSession() {
972      throw unsupportedOp();
973   }
974
975   /**
976    * Returns the annotation provider for this context.
977    *
978    * @return The annotation provider for this context.
979    */
980   public AnnotationProvider getAnnotationProvider() { return annotationProvider; }
981
982   /**
983    * Returns a session to use for this context.
984    *
985    * <p>
986    * Note that subclasses may opt to return a reusable non-modifiable session.
987    *
988    * @return A new session object.
989    */
990   public ContextSession getSession() { return createSession().build(); }
991
992   /**
993    * Debug mode.
994    *
995    * @see Context.Builder#debug()
996    * @return
997    *    <jk>true</jk> if debug mode is enabled.
998    */
999   public boolean isDebug() { return debug; }
1000
1001   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
1002   public String toString() {
1003      return r(properties());
1004   }
1005
1006   /**
1007    * Perform optional initialization on builder before it is used.
1008    *
1009    * <p>
1010    * Default behavior is a no-op.
1011    *
1012    * @param builder The builder to initialize.
1013    */
1014   protected void init(Builder builder) {}
1015
1016   /**
1017    * Returns the properties on this bean as a map for debugging.
1018    *
1019    * @return The properties on this bean as a map for debugging.
1020    */
1021   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
1022      return filteredBeanPropertyMap()
1023         .a(PROP_annotations, annotations)
1024         .a(PROP_debug, debug);
1025   }
1026}
